headline: National Debt Hits RM1.25 Trillion Milestone in 2025
context: Malaysia's federal government debt reached RM1.25 trillion or 64.3% of GDP by end-2025, approaching the self-imposed statutory ceiling of 65% set under the revised fiscal framework.

cards.big: RM1.25 trillion in debt means every Malaysian owes RM37,500 — including newborns
cards.sub: Debt servicing costs RM51 billion in 2025, exceeding combined education development spending

cards.big: Debt service charges consume 16.8% of federal revenue — up from 12.4% in 2019
cards.sub: Every 1% interest rate rise adds RM8.5 billion to annual debt servicing costs

cards.big: Government guarantees add another RM380 billion in contingent liabilities from GLCs and agencies
cards.sub: Total fiscal exposure including guarantees reaches 83.7% of GDP — among highest in Asia

cards.big: Parliament's 65% debt ceiling was raised from 60% in 2021 and 55% in 2020 without public referendum
cards.sub: Statutory ceiling loses credibility when government simply raises it each time debt approaches the limit

cards.big: Raising the debt ceiling isn't fiscal management — it's moving the goalposts while the debt clock keeps ticking
Should Malaysia adopt an independent fiscal council with veto power over debt ceiling changes?
